translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, a called apostle, having been separated to the good news of God --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which He announced before through His prophets in holy writings --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: concerning His Son, (who is come of the seed of David according to the flesh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who is marked out Son of God in power, according to the Spirit of sanctification, by the rising again from the dead,) Jesus Christ our Lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: through whom we did receive grace and apostleship, for obedience of faith among all the nations, in behalf of his name;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: among whom are also ye, the called of Jesus Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to all who are in Rome, beloved of God, called saints; Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father, and [from] the Lord Jesus Christ!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: first, indeed, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, that your faith is proclaimed in the whole world;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for God is my witness, whom I serve in my spirit in the good news of His Son, how unceasingly I make mention of you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: always in my prayers beseeching, if by any means now at length I shall have a prosperous journey, by the will of God, to come unto you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift, that ye may be established;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and that is, that I may be comforted together among you, through the faith in one another, both yours and mine.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I do not wish you to be ignorant, brethren, that many times I did purpose to come unto you -- and was hindered till the present time -- that some fruit I might have also among you, even as also among the other nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Both to Greeks and to foreigners, both to wise and to thoughtless, I am a debtor,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so, as much as in me is, I am ready also to you who [are] in Rome to proclaim good news,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I am not ashamed of the good news of the Christ, for it is the power of God to salvation to every one who is believing, both to Jew first, and to Greek.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the righteousness of God in it is revealed from faith to faith, according as it hath been written, 'And the righteous one by faith shall live,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for revealed is the wrath of God from heaven upon all impiety and unrighteousness of men, holding down the truth in unrighteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because that which is known of God is manifest among them, for God did manifest [it] to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the invisible things of Him from the creation of the world, by the things made being understood, are plainly seen, both His eternal power and Godhead -- to their being inexcusable;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because, having known God they did not glorify [Him] as God, nor gave thanks, but were made vain in their reasonings, and their unintelligent heart was darkened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: professing to be wise, they were made fools,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and changed the glory of the incorruptible God into the likeness of an image of corruptible man, and of fowls, and of quadrupeds, and of reptiles.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore also God did give them up, in the desires of their hearts, to uncleanness, to dishonour their bodies among themselves;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who did change the truth of God into a falsehood, and did honour and serve the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed to the ages. Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of this did God give them up to dishonourable affections, for even their females did change the natural use into that against nature;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in like manner also the males having left the natural use of the female, did burn in their longing toward one another; males with males working shame, and the recompense of their error that was fit, in themselves receiving.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, according as they did not approve of having God in knowledge, God gave them up to a disapproved mind, to do the things not seemly;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having been filled with all unrighteousness, whoredom, wickedness, covetousness, malice; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, evil dispositions; whisperers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: evil-speakers, God-haters, insulting, proud, boasters, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: unintelligent, faithless, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who the righteous judgment of God having known -- that those practising such things are worthy of death -- not only do them, but also have delight with those practising them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore, thou art inexcusable, O man -- every one who is judging -- for in that in which thou dost judge the other, thyself thou dost condemn, for the same things thou dost practise who art judging,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we have known that the judgment of God is according to truth, upon those practising such things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And dost thou think this, O man, who art judging those who such things are practising, and art doing them, that thou shalt escape the judgment of God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or the riches of His goodness, and forbearance, and long-suffering, dost thou despise? -- not knowing that the goodness of God doth lead thee to reformation!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but, according to thy hardness and impenitent heart, thou dost treasure up to thyself wrath, in a day of wrath and of the revelation of the righteous judgment of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who shall render to each according to his works;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to those, indeed, who in continuance of a good work, do seek glory, and honour, and incorruptibility -- life age-during;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to those contentious, and disobedient, indeed, to the truth, and obeying the unrighteousness -- indignation and wrath,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: tribulation and distress, upon every soul of man that is working the evil, both of Jew first, and of Greek;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and glory, and honour, and peace, to every one who is working the good, both to Jew first, and to Greek.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For there is no acceptance of faces with God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as many as without law did sin, without law also shall perish, and as many as did sin in law, through law shall be judged,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for not the hearers of the law [are] righteous before God, but the doers of the law shall be declared righteous: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, when nations that have not a law, by nature may do the things of the law, these not having a law -- to themselves are a law;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who do shew the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience also witnessing with them, and between one another the thoughts accusing or else defending,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the day when God shall judge the secrets of men, according to my good news, through Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, thou art named a Jew, and dost rest upon the law, and dost boast in God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and dost know the will, and dost approve the distinctions, being instructed out of the law,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and hast confidence that thou thyself art a leader of blind ones, a light of those in darkness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: an instructor of foolish ones, a teacher of babes, having the form of the knowledge and of the truth in the law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou, then, who art teaching another, thyself dost thou not teach?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou who art preaching not to steal, dost thou steal? thou who art saying not to commit adultery, dost thou commit adultery? thou who art abhorring the idols, dost thou rob temples?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou who in the law dost boast, through the transgression of the law God dost thou dishonour?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the name of God because of you is evil spoken of among the nations, according as it hath been written.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For circumcision, indeed, doth profit, if law thou mayest practise, but if a transgressor of law thou mayest be, thy circumcision hath become uncircumcision.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If, therefore the uncircumcision the righteousness of the law may keep, shall not his uncircumcision for circumcision be reckoned?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the uncircumcision, by nature, fulfilling the law, shall judge thee who, through letter and circumcision, [art] a transgressor of law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he is not a Jew who is [so] outwardly, neither [is] circumcision that which is outward in flesh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but a Jew [is] he who is [so] inwardly, and circumcision [is] of the heart, in spirit, not in letter, of which the praise is not of men, but of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then, [is] the superiority of the Jew? or what the profit of the circumcision?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: much in every way; for first, indeed, that they were intrusted with the oracles of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for what, if certain were faithless? shall their faithlessness the faithfulness of god make useless?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let it not be! and let God become true, and every man false, according as it hath been written, 'That Thou mayest be declared righteous in Thy words, and mayest overcome in Thy being judged.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, if our unrighteousness God's righteousness doth establish, what shall we say? is God unrighteous who is inflicting the wrath? (after the manner of a man I speak)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let it not be! since how shall God judge the world?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if the truth of God in my falsehood did more abound to His glory, why yet am I also as a sinner judged?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not, as we are evil spoken of, and as certain affirm us to say -- 'We may do the evil things, that the good ones may come?' whose judgment is righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then? are we better? not at all! for we did before charge both Jews and Greeks with being all under sin,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according as it hath been written -- 'There is none righteous, not even one;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is none who is understanding, there is none who is seeking after God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All did go out of the way, together they became unprofitable, there is none doing good, there is not even one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A sepulchre opened [is] their throat; with their tongues they used deceit; poison of asps [is] under their lips.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Swift [are] their feet to shed blood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ruin and misery [are] in their ways.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a way of peace they did not know.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is no fear of God before their eyes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we have known that as many things as the law saith, to those in the law it doth speak, that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may come under judgment to God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore by works of law shall no flesh be declared righteous before Him, for through law is a knowledge of sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now apart from law hath the righteousness of God been manifested, testified to by the law and the prophets,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the righteousness of God [is] through the faith of Jesus Christ to all, and upon all those believing, -- for there is no difference,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for all did sin, and are come short of the glory of God --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: being declared righteous freely by His grace through the redemption that [is] in Christ Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom God did set forth a mercy seat, through the faith in his blood, for the shewing forth of His righteousness, because of the passing over of the bygone sins in the forbearance of God --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the shewing forth of His righteousness in the present time, for His being righteous, and declaring him righteous who [is] of the faith of Jesus.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Where then [is] the boasting? it was excluded; by what law? of works? no, but by a law of faith:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore do we reckon a man to be declared righteous by faith, apart from works of law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The God of Jews only [is He], and not also of nations?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yes, also of nations; since one [is] God who shall declare righteous the circumcision by faith, and the uncircumcision through the faith.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Law then do we make useless through the faith? let it not be! yea, we do establish law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then, shall we say Abraham our father, to have found, according to flesh?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if Abraham by works was declared righteous, he hath to boast -- but not before God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for what doth the writing say? 'And Abraham did believe God, and it was reckoned to him -- to righteousness;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to him who is working, the reward is not reckoned of grace, but of debt;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to him who is not working, and is believing upon Him who is declaring righteous the impious, his faith is reckoned -- to righteousness:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: even as David also doth speak of the happiness of the man to whom God doth reckon righteousness apart from works:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy they whose lawless acts were forgiven, and whose sins were covered;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: happy the man to whom the Lord may not reckon sin.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: [Is] this happiness, then, upon the circumcision, or also upon the uncircumcision -- for we say that the faith was reckoned to Abraham -- to righteousness?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how then was it reckoned? he being in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a sign he did receive of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith in the uncircumcision, for his being father of all those believing through uncircumcision, for the righteousness also being reckoned to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and father of circumcision to those not of circumcision only, but who also walk in the steps of the faith, that [is] in the uncircumcision of our father Abraham.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For not through law [is] the promise to Abraham, or to his seed, of his being heir of the world, but through the righteousness of faith;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if they who are of law [are] heirs, the faith hath been made void, and the promise hath been made useless;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the law doth work wrath; for where law is not, neither [is] transgression.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of this [it is] of faith, that [it may be] according to grace, for the promise being sure to all the seed, not to that which [is] of the law only, but also to that which [is] of the faith of Abraham,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who is father of us all (according as it hath been written -- 'A father of many nations I have set thee,') before Him whom he did believe -- God, who is quickening the dead, and is calling the things that be not as being.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who, against hope in hope did believe, for his becoming father of many nations according to that spoken: 'So shall thy seed be;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not having been weak in the faith, he did not consider his own body, already become dead, (being about a hundred years old,) and the deadness of Sarah's womb,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and at the promise of God did not stagger in unbelief, but was strengthened in faith, having given glory to God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having been fully persuaded that what He hath promised He is able also to do:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore also it was reckoned to him to righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it was not written on his account alone, that it was reckoned to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but also on ours, to whom it is about to be reckoned -- to us believing on Him who did raise up Jesus our Lord out of the dead,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who was delivered up because of our offences, and was raised up because of our being declared righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Having been declared righteous, then, by faith, we have peace toward God through our Lord Jesus Christ,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: through whom also we have the access by the faith into this grace in which we have stood, and we boast on the hope of the glory of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And not only [so], but we also boast in the tribulations, knowing that the tribulation doth work endurance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the endurance, experience; and the experience, hope;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the hope doth not make ashamed, because the love of God hath been poured forth in our hearts through the Holy Spirit that hath been given to us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For in our being still ailing, Christ in due time did die for the impious;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for scarcely for a righteous man will any one die, for for the good man perhaps some one also doth dare to die;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and God doth commend His own love to us, that, in our being still sinners, Christ did die for us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: much more, then, having been declared righteous now in his blood, we shall be saved through him from the wrath;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if, being enemies, we have been reconciled to God through the death of His Son, much more, having been reconciled, we shall be saved in his life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And not only [so], but we are also boasting in God, through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom now we did receive the reconciliation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because of this, even as through one man the sin did enter into the world, and through the sin the death; and thus to all men the death did pass through, for that all did sin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for till law sin was in the world: and sin is not reckoned when there is not law;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the death did reign from Adam till Moses, even upon those not having sinned in the likeness of Adam's transgression, who is a type of him who is coming.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But, not as the offence so also [is] the free gift; for if by the offence of the one the many did die, much more did the grace of God, and the free gift in grace of the one man Jesus Christ, abound to the many;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not as through one who did sin [is] the free gift, for the judgment indeed [is] of one to condemnation, but the gift [is] of many offences to a declaration of 'Righteous,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if by the offence of the one the death did reign through the one, much more those, who the abundance of the grace and of the free gift of the righteousness are receiving, in life shall reign through the one -- Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So, then, as through one offence to all men [it is] to condemnation, so also through one declaration of 'Righteous' [it is] to all men to justification of life;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as through the disobedience of the one man, the many were constituted sinners: so also through the obedience of the one, shall the many be constituted righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And law came in, that the offence might abound, and where the sin did abound, the grace did overabound,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that even as the sin did reign in the death, so also the grace may reign, through righteousness, to life age-during, through Jesus Christ our Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then, shall we say? shall we continue in the sin that the grace may abound?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let it not be! we who died to the sin -- how shall we still live in it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: are ye ignorant that we, as many as were baptized to Christ Jesus, to his death were baptized?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: we were buried together, then, with him through the baptism to the death, that even as Christ was raised up out of the dead through the glory of the Father, so also we in newness of life might walk.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, if we have become planted together to the likeness of his death, [so] also we shall be of the rising again;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this knowing, that our old man was crucified with [him], that the body of the sin may be made useless, for our no longer serving the sin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he who hath died hath been set free from the sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if we died with Christ, we believe that we also shall live with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: knowing that Christ, having been raised up out of the dead, doth no more die, death over him hath no more lordship;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for in that he died, to the sin he died once, and in that he liveth, he liveth to God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so also ye, reckon yourselves to be dead indeed to the sin, and living to God in Jesus Christ our Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let not then the sin reign in your mortal body, to obey it in its desires;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: neither present ye your members instruments of unrighteousness to the sin, but present yourselves to God as living out of the dead, and your members instruments of righteousness to God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for sin over you shall not have lordship, for ye are not under law, but under grace.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What then? shall we sin because we are not under law but under grace? let it not be!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: have ye not known that to whom ye present yourselves servants for obedience, servants ye are to him to whom ye obey, whether of sin to death, or of obedience to righteousness?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and thanks to God, that ye were servants of the sin, and -- were obedient from the heart to the form of teaching to which ye were delivered up;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having been freed from the sin, ye became servants to the righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the manner of men I speak, because of the weakness of your flesh, for even as ye did present your members servants to the uncleanness and to the lawlessness -- to the lawlessness, so now present your members servants to the righteousness -- to sanctification,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for when ye were servants of the sin, ye were free from the righteousness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: what fruit, therefore, were ye having then, in the things of which ye are now ashamed? for the end of those [is] death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, having been freed from the sin, and having become servants to God, ye have your fruit -- to sanctification, and the end life age-during;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the wages of the sin [is] death, and the gift of God [is] life age-during in Christ Jesus our Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Are ye ignorant, brethren -- for to those knowing law I speak -- that the law hath lordship over the man as long as he liveth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the married woman to the living husband hath been bound by law, and if the husband may die, she hath been free from the law of the husband;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so, then, the husband being alive, an adulteress she shall be called if she may become another man's; and if the husband may die, she is free from the law, so as not to be an adulteress, having become another man's.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So that, my brethren, ye also were made dead to the law through the body of the Christ, for your becoming another's, who out of the dead was raised up, that we might bear fruit to God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for when we were in the flesh, the passions of the sins, that [are] through the law, were working in our members, to bear fruit to the death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now we have ceased from the law, that being dead in which we were held, so that we may serve in newness of spirit, and not in oldness of letter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then, shall we say? the law [is] sin? let it not be! but the sin I did not know except through law, for also the covetousness I had not known if the law had not said:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thou shalt not covet;' and the sin having received an opportunity, through the command, did work in me all covetousness -- for apart from law sin is dead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I was alive apart from law once, and the command having come, the sin revived, and I died;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the command that [is] for life, this was found by me for death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the sin, having received an opportunity, through the command, did deceive me, and through it did slay [me];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that the law, indeed, [is] holy, and the command holy, and righteous, and good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That which is good then, to me hath it become death? let it not be! but the sin, that it might appear sin, through the good, working death to me, that the sin might become exceeding sinful through the command,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for we have known that the law is spiritual, and I am fleshly, sold by the sin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for that which I work, I do not acknowledge; for not what I will, this I practise, but what I hate, this I do.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if what I do not will, this I do, I consent to the law that [it is] good,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now it is no longer I that work it, but the sin dwelling in me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I have known that there doth not dwell in me, that is, in my flesh, good: for to will is present with me, and to work that which is right I do not find,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the good that I will, I do not; but the evil that I do not will, this I practise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if what I do not will, this I do, it is no longer I that work it, but the sin that is dwelling in me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I find, then, the law, that when I desire to do what is right, with me the evil is present,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I delight in the law of God according to the inward man,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I behold another law in my members, warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity to the law of the sin that [is] in my members.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A wretched man I [am]! who shall deliver me out of the body of this death?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I thank God -- through Jesus Christ our Lord; so then, I myself indeed with the mind do serve the law of God, and with the flesh, the law of sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is, then, now no condemnation to those in Christ Jesus, who walk not according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the law of the Spirit of the life in Christ Jesus did set me free from the law of the sin and of the death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for what the law was not able to do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God, His own Son having sent in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, did condemn the sin in the flesh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that the righteousness of the law may be fulfilled in us, who do not walk according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For those who are according to the flesh, the things of the flesh do mind; and those according to the Spirit, the things of the Spirit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the mind of the flesh [is] death, and the mind of the Spirit -- life and peace;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because the mind of the flesh [is] enmity to God, for to the law of God it doth not subject itself,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for neither is it able; and those who are in the flesh are not able to please God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And ye are not in the flesh, but in the Spirit, if indeed the Spirit of God doth dwell in you; and if any one hath not the Spirit of Christ -- this one is not His;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if Christ [is] in you, the body, indeed, [is] dead because of sin, and the Spirit [is] life because of righteousness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if the Spirit of Him who did raise up Jesus out of the dead doth dwell in you, He who did raise up the Christ out of the dead shall quicken also your dying bodies, through His Spirit dwelling in you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So, then, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live according to the flesh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if according to the flesh ye do live, ye are about to die; and if, by the Spirit, the deeds of the body ye put to death, ye shall live;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are the sons of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for ye did not receive a spirit of bondage again for fear, but ye did receive a spirit of adoption in which we cry, 'Abba -- Father.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Spirit himself doth testify with our spirit, that we are children of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if children, also heirs, heirs, indeed, of God, and heirs together of Christ -- if, indeed, we suffer together, that we may also be glorified together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I reckon that the sufferings of the present time [are] not worthy [to be compared] with the glory about to be revealed in us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the earnest looking out of the creation doth expect the revelation of the sons of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for to vanity was the creation made subject -- not of its will, but because of Him who did subject [it] -- in hope,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that also the creation itself shall be set free from the servitude of the corruption to the liberty of the glory of the children of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for we have known that all the creation doth groan together, and doth travail in pain together till now.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And not only [so], but also we ourselves, having the first-fruit of the Spirit, we also ourselves in ourselves do groan, adoption expecting -- the redemption of our body;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for in hope we were saved, and hope beheld is not hope; for what any one doth behold, why also doth he hope for [it]?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if what we do not behold we hope for, through continuance we expect [it].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, in like manner also, the Spirit doth help our weaknesses; for, what we may pray for, as it behoveth [us], we have not known, but the Spirit himself doth make intercession for us with groanings unutterable,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He who is searching the hearts hath known what [is] the mind of the Spirit, because according to God he doth intercede for saints.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we have known that to those loving God all things do work together for good, to those who are called according to purpose;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because whom He did foreknow, He also did fore-appoint, conformed to the image of His Son, that he might be first-born among many brethren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whom He did fore-appoint, these also He did call; and whom He did call, these also He declared righteous; and whom He declared righteous, these also He did glorify.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then, shall we say unto these things? if God [is] for us, who [is] against us?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He who indeed His own Son did not spare, but for us all did deliver him up, how shall He not also with him the all things grant to us?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who shall lay a charge against the choice ones of God? God [is] He that is declaring righteous,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who [is] he that is condemning? Christ [is] He that died, yea, rather also, was raised up; who is also on the right hand of God -- who also doth intercede for us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who shall separate us from the love of the Christ? tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (according as it hath been written -- 'For Thy sake we are put to death all the day long, we were reckoned as sheep of slaughter,')

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but in all these we more than conquer, through him who loved us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I am persuaded that neither death, nor life, nor messengers, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor things about to be, nor height, nor depth, nor any other created thing, shall be able to separate us from the love of god, that [is] in Christ Jesus our Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Truth I say in Christ, I lie not, my conscience bearing testimony with me in the Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that I have great grief and unceasing pain in my heart --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I was wishing, I myself, to be anathema from the Christ -- for my brethren, my kindred, according to the flesh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who are Israelites, whose [is] the adoption, and the glory, and the covenants, and the lawgiving, and the service, and the promises,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whose [are] the fathers, and of whom [is] the Christ, according to the flesh, who is over all, God blessed to the ages. Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it is not possible that the word of God hath failed; for not all who [are] of Israel are these Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor because they are seed of Abraham [are] all children, but -- 'in Isaac shall a seed be called to thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that is, the children of the flesh -- these [are] not children of God; but the children of the promise are reckoned for seed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the word of promise [is] this; 'According to this time I will come, and there shall be to Sarah a son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And not only [so], but also Rebecca, having conceived by one -- Isaac our father --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (for they being not yet born, neither having done anything good or evil, that the purpose of God, according to choice, might remain; not of works, but of Him who is calling,) it was said to her --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The greater shall serve the less;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according as it hath been written, 'Jacob I did love, and Esau I did hate.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then, shall we say? unrighteousness [is] with God? let it not be!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for to Moses He saith, 'I will do kindness to whom I do kindness, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so, then -- not of him who is willing, nor of him who is running, but of God who is doing kindness:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the Writing saith to Pharaoh -- 'For this very thing I did raise thee up, that I might shew in thee My power, and that My name might be declared in all the land;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so, then, to whom He willeth, He doth kindness, and to whom He willeth, He doth harden.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou wilt say, then, to me, 'Why yet doth He find fault? for His counsel who hath resisted?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nay, but, O man, who art thou that art answering again to God? shall the thing formed say to Him who did form [it], Why me didst thou make thus?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: hath not the potter authority over the clay, out of the same lump to make the one vessel to honour, and the one to dishonour?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if God, willing to shew the wrath and to make known His power, did endure, in much long suffering, vessels of wrath fitted for destruction,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and that He might make known the riches of His glory on vessels of kindness, that He before prepared for glory, whom also He did call -- us --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: not only out of Jews, but also out of nations,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as also in Hosea He saith, 'I will call what [is] not My people -- My people; and her not beloved -- Beloved,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it shall be -- in the place where it was said to them, Ye [are] not My people; there they shall be called sons of the living God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Isaiah doth cry concerning Israel, 'If the number of the sons of Israel may be as the sand of the sea, the remnant shall be saved;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for a matter He is finishing, and is cutting short in righteousness, because a matter cut short will the Lord do upon the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and according as Isaiah saith before, 'Except the Lord of Sabaoth did leave to us a seed, as Sodom we had become, and as Gomorrah we had been made like.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What, then, shall we say? that nations who are not pursuing righteousness did attain to righteousness, and righteousness that [is] of faith,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Israel, pursuing a law of righteousness, at a law of righteousness did not arrive;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore? because -- not by faith, but as by works of law; for they did stumble at the stone of stumbling,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according as it hath been written, 'Lo, I place in Sion a stone of stumbling and a rock of offence; and every one who is believing thereon shall not be ashamed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Brethren, the pleasure indeed of my heart, and my supplication that [is] to God for Israel, is -- for salvation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I bear them testimony that they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for not knowing the righteousness of God, and their own righteousness seeking to establish, to the righteousness of God they did not submit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Christ is an end of law for righteousness to every one who is believing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for Moses doth describe the righteousness that [is] of the law, that, 'The man who did them shall live in them,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the righteousness of faith doth thus speak: 'Thou mayest not say in thine heart, Who shall go up to the heaven,' that is, Christ to bring down?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or, 'Who shall go down to the abyss,' that is, Christ out of the dead to bring up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But what doth it say? 'Nigh thee is the saying -- in thy mouth, and in thy heart:' that is, the saying of the faith, that we preach;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that if thou mayest confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and mayest believe in thy heart that God did raise him out of the dead, thou shalt be saved,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for with the heart doth [one] believe to righteousness, and with the mouth is confession made to salvation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the Writing saith, 'Every one who is believing on him shall not be ashamed,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for there is no difference between Jew and Greek, for the same Lord of all [is] rich to all those calling upon Him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for every one -- whoever shall call upon the name of the Lord, he shall be saved.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How then shall they call upon [him] in whom they did not believe? and how shall they believe [on him] of whom they did not hear? and how shall they hear apart from one preaching?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and how shall they preach, if they may not be sent? according as it hath been written, 'How beautiful the feet of those proclaiming good tidings of peace, of those proclaiming good tidings of the good things!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But they were not all obedient to the good tidings, for Isaiah saith, 'Lord, who did give credence to our report?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so then the faith [is] by a report, and the report through a saying of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I say, Did they not hear? yes, indeed -- 'to all the earth their voice went forth, and to the ends of the habitable world their sayings.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But I say, Did not Israel know? first Moses saith, 'I will provoke you to jealousy by [that which is] not a nation; by an unintelligent nation I will anger you,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Isaiah is very bold, and saith, 'I was found by those not seeking Me; I became manifest to those not inquiring after Me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and unto Israel He saith, 'All the day I did stretch out My hands unto a people unbelieving and gainsaying.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I say, then, Did God cast away His people? let it not be! for I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God did not cast away His people whom He knew before; have ye not known -- in Elijah -- what the Writing saith? how he doth plead with God concerning Israel, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lord, Thy prophets they did kill, and Thy altars they dug down, and I was left alone, and they seek my life;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but what saith the divine answer to him? 'I left to Myself seven thousand men, who did not bow a knee to Baal.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So then also in the present time a remnant according to the choice of grace there hath been;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if by grace, no more of works, otherwise the grace becometh no more grace; and if of works, it is no more grace, otherwise the work is no more work.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What then? What Israel doth seek after, this it did not obtain, and the chosen did obtain, and the rest were hardened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according as it hath been written, 'God gave to them a spirit of deep sleep, eyes not to see, and ears not to hear,' -- unto this very day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and David saith, 'Let their table become for a snare, and for a trap, and for a stumbling-block, and for a recompense to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let their eyes be darkened -- not to behold, and their back do Thou always bow down.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I say, then, Did they stumble that they might fall? let it not be! but by their fall the salvation [is] to the nations, to arouse them to jealousy;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if the fall of them [is] the riches of a world, and the diminution of them the riches of nations, how much more the fulness of them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For to you I speak -- to the nations -- inasmuch as I am indeed an apostle of nations, my ministration I do glorify;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if by any means I shall arouse to jealousy mine own flesh, and shall save some of them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if the casting away of them [is] a reconciliation of the world, what the reception -- if not life out of the dead?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if the first-fruit [is] holy, the lump also; and if the root [is] holy, the branches also.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if certain of the branches were broken off, and thou, being a wild olive tree, wast graffed in among them, and a fellow-partaker of the root and of the fatness of the olive tree didst become --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: do not boast against the branches; and if thou dost boast, thou dost not bear the root, but the root thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou wilt say, then, 'The branches were broken off, that I might be graffed in;' right!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: by unbelief they were broken off, and thou hast stood by faith; be not high-minded, but be fearing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if God the natural branches did not spare -- lest perhaps He also shall not spare thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, then, goodness and severity of God -- upon those indeed who fell, severity; and upon thee, goodness, if thou mayest remain in the goodness, otherwise, thou also shalt be cut off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those also, if they may not remain in unbelief, shall be graffed in, for God is able again to graff them in;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for if thou, out of the olive tree, wild by nature, wast cut out, and, contrary to nature, wast graffed into a good olive tree, how much rather shall they, who [are] according to nature, be graffed into their own olive tree?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I do not wish you to be ignorant, brethren, of this secret -- that ye may not be wise in your own conceits -- that hardness in part to Israel hath happened till the fulness of the nations may come in;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and so all Israel shall be saved, according as it hath been written, 'There shall come forth out of Sion he who is delivering, and he shall turn away impiety from Jacob,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this to them [is] the covenant from Me, when I may take away their sins.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As regards, indeed, the good tidings, [they are] enemies on your account; and as regards the choice -- beloved on account of the fathers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for unrepented of [are] the gifts and the calling of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as ye also once did not believe in God, and now did find kindness by the unbelief of these:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so also these now did not believe, that in your kindness they also may find kindness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for God did shut up together the whole to unbelief, that to the whole He might do kindness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O depth of riches, and wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable His judgments, and untraceable His ways!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for who did know the mind of the Lord? or who did become His counsellor?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or who did first give to Him, and it shall be given back to him again?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because of Him, and through Him, and to Him [are] the all things; to Him [is] the glory -- to the ages. Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I call upon you, therefore, brethren, through the compassions of God, to present your bodies a sacrifice -- living, sanctified, acceptable to God -- your intelligent service;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and be not conformed to this age, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, for your proving what [is] the will of God -- the good, and acceptable, and perfect.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I say, through the grace that was given to me, to every one who is among you, not to think above what it behoveth to think; but to think so as to think wisely, as to each God did deal a measure of faith,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as in one body we have many members, and all the members have not the same office,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so we, the many, one body are in Christ, and members each one of one another.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gifts, different according to the grace that was given to us; whether prophecy -- 'According to the proportion of faith!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or ministration -- 'In the ministration!' or he who is teaching -- 'In the teaching!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or he who is exhorting -- 'In the exhortation!' he who is sharing -- 'In simplicity!' he who is leading -- 'In diligence?' he who is doing kindness -- 'In cheerfulness.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The love unfeigned: abhorring the evil; cleaving to the good;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the love of brethren, to one another kindly affectioned: in the honour going before one another;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the diligence not slothful; in the spirit fervent; the Lord serving;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the hope rejoicing; in the tribulation enduring; in the prayer persevering;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to the necessities of the saints communicating; the hospitality pursuing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bless those persecuting you; bless, and curse not;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to rejoice with the rejoicing, and to weep with the weeping,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: of the same mind one toward another, not minding the high things, but with the lowly going along; become not wise in your own conceit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: giving back to no one evil for evil; providing right things before all men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If possible -- so far as in you -- with all men being in peace;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: not avenging yourselves, beloved, but give place to the wrath, for it hath been written, 'Vengeance [is] Mine,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I will recompense again, saith the Lord;' if, then, thine enemy doth hunger, feed him; if he doth thirst, give him drink; for this doing, coals of fire thou shalt heap upon his head;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be not overcome by the evil, but overcome, in the good, the evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let every soul to the higher authorities be subject, for there is no authority except from God, and the authorities existing are appointed by God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that he who is setting himself against the authority, against God's ordinance hath resisted; and those resisting, to themselves shall receive judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For those ruling are not a terror to the good works, but to the evil; and dost thou wish not to be afraid of the authority? that which is good be doing, and thou shalt have praise from it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for of God it is a ministrant to thee for good; and if that which is evil thou mayest do, be fearing, for not in vain doth it bear the sword; for of God it is a ministrant, an avenger for wrath to him who is doing that which is evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore it is necessary to be subject, not only because of the wrath, but also because of the conscience,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for because of this also pay ye tribute; for servants of God they are, on this very thing attending continually;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: render, therefore, to all [their] dues; to whom tribute, the tribute; to whom custom, the custom; to whom fear, the fear; to whom honour, the honour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To no one owe anything, except to love one another; for he who is loving the other -- law he hath fulfilled,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, 'Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt do no murder, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false testimony, Thou shalt not covet;' and if there is any other command, in this word it is summed up, in this: 'Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the love to the neighbour doth work no ill; the love, therefore, [is] the fulness of law.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this, knowing the time, that for us, the hour already [is] to be aroused out of sleep, for now nearer [is] our salvation than when we did believe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the night did advance, and the day came nigh; let us lay aside, therefore, the works of the darkness, and let us put on the armour of the light;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as in day-time, let us walk becomingly; not in revellings and drunkennesses, not in chamberings and lasciviousnesses, not in strife and emulation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and for the flesh take no forethought -- for desires.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And him who is weak in the faith receive ye -- not to determinations of reasonings;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: one doth believe that he may eat all things -- and he who is weak doth eat herbs;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let not him who is eating despise him who is not eating: and let not him who is not eating judge him who is eating, for God did receive him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou -- who art thou that art judging another's domestic? to his own master he doth stand or fall; and he shall be made to stand, for God is able to make him stand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: One doth judge one day above another, and another doth judge every day [alike]; let each in his own mind be fully assured.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He who is regarding the day, to the Lord he doth regard [it], and he who is not regarding the day, to the Lord he doth not regard [it]. He who is eating, to the Lord he doth eat, for he doth give thanks to God; and he who is not eating, to the Lord he doth not eat, and doth give thanks to God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For none of us to himself doth live, and none to himself doth die;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for both, if we may live, to the Lord we live; if also we may die, to the Lord we die; both then if we may live, also if we may die, we are the Lord's;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for because of this Christ both died and rose again, and lived again, that both of dead and of living he may be Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou, why dost thou judge thy brother? or again, thou, why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand at the tribunal of the Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it hath been written, 'I live! saith the Lord -- to Me bow shall every knee, and every tongue shall confess to God;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so, then, each of us concerning himself shall give reckoning to God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: no longer, therefore, may we judge one another, but this judge ye rather, not to put a stumbling-stone before the brother, or an offence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have known, and am persuaded, in the Lord Jesus, that nothing [is] unclean of itself, except to him who is reckoning anything to be unclean -- to that one [it is] unclean;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if through victuals thy brother is grieved, no more dost thou walk according to love; do not with thy victuals destroy that one for whom Christ died.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let not, then, your good be evil spoken of,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the reign of God is not eating and drinking, but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he who in these things is serving the Christ, [is] acceptable to God and approved of men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So, then, the things of peace may we pursue, and the things of building up one another;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the sake of victuals cast not down the work of God; all things, indeed, [are] pure, but evil [is] to the man who is eating through stumbling.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Right [it is] not to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor to [do anything] in which thy brother doth stumble, or is made to fall, or is weak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast faith! to thyself have [it] before God; happy is he who is not judging himself in what he doth approve,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who is making a difference, if he may eat, hath been condemned, because [it is] not of faith; and all that [is] not of faith is sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we ought -- we who are strong -- to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for let each one of us please the neighbour for good, unto edification,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for even the Christ did not please himself, but, according as it hath been written, 'The reproaches of those reproaching Thee fell upon me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, as many things as were written before, for our instruction were written before, that through the endurance, and the exhortation of the Writings, we might have the hope.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And may the God of the endurance, and of the exhortation, give to you to have the same mind toward one another, according to Christ Jesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that with one accord -- with one mouth -- ye may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore receive ye one another, according as also the Christ did receive us, to the glory of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I say Jesus Christ to have become a ministrant of circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises to the fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the nations for kindness to glorify God, according as it hath been written, 'Because of this I will confess to Thee among nations, and to Thy name I will sing praise,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and again it saith, 'Rejoice ye nations, with His people;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and again, 'Praise the Lord, all ye nations; and laud Him, all ye peoples;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and again, Isaiah saith, 'There shall be the root of Jesse, and he who is rising to rule nations -- upon him shall nations hope;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the God of the hope shall fill you with all joy and peace in the believing, for your abounding in the hope in power of the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I am persuaded, my brethren -- I myself also -- concerning you, that ye yourselves also are full of goodness, having been filled with all knowledge, able also one another to admonish;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the more boldly I did write to you, brethren, in part, as putting you in mind, because of the grace that is given to me by God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for my being a servant of Jesus Christ to the nations, acting as priest in the good news of God, that the offering up of the nations may become acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have, then, a boasting in Christ Jesus, in the things pertaining to God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I will not dare to speak anything of the things that Christ did not work through me, to obedience of nations, by word and deed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in power of signs and wonders, in power of the Spirit of God; so that I, from Jerusalem, and in a circle as far as Illyricum, have fully preached the good news of the Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and so counting it honour to proclaim good news, not where Christ was named -- that upon another's foundation I might not build --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but according as it hath been written, 'To whom it was not told concerning him, they shall see; and they who have not heard, shall understand.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore, also, I was hindered many times from coming unto you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now, no longer having place in these parts, and having a longing to come unto you for many years,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when I may go on to Spain I will come unto you, for I hope in going through, to see you, and by you to be set forward thither, if of you first, in part, I shall be filled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, now, I go on to Jerusalem, ministering to the saints;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it pleased Macedonia and Achaia well to make a certain contribution for the poor of the saints who [are] in Jerusalem;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it pleased well, and their debtors they are, for if in their spiritual things the nations did participate, they ought also, in the fleshly things, to minister to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This, then, having finished, and having sealed to them this fruit, I will return through you, to Spain;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I have known that coming unto you -- in the fulness of the blessing of the good news of Christ I shall come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I call upon you, brethren, through our Lord Jesus Christ, and through the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in the prayers for me unto God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that I may be delivered from those not believing in Judea, and that my ministration, that [is] for Jerusalem, may become acceptable to the saints;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that in joy I may come unto you, through the will of God, and may be refreshed with you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the God of the peace [be] with you all. Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I commend you to Phebe our sister -- being a ministrant of the assembly that [is] in Cenchrea --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that ye may receive her in the Lord, as doth become saints, and may assist her in whatever matter she may have need of you -- for she also became a leader of many, and of myself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salute Priscilla and Aquilas, my fellow-workmen in Christ Jesus --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who for my life their own neck did lay down, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the nations --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the assembly at their house; salute Epaenetus, my beloved, who is first-fruit of Achaia to Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salute Mary, who did labour much for us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute Andronicus and Junias, my kindred, and my fellow-captives, who are of note among the apostles, who also have been in Christ before me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salute Amplias, my beloved in the Lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute Arbanus, our fellow-workman in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute Apelles, the approved in Christ; salute those of the [household] of Aristobulus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute Herodion, my kinsman; salute those of the [household] of Narcissus, who are in the Lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute Tryphaena, and Tryphosa, who are labouring in the Lord; salute Persis, the beloved, who did labour much in the Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salute Rufus, the choice one in the Lord, and his mother and mine,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren with them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute Philologus, and Julias, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints with them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute one another in a holy kiss; the assemblies of Christ do salute you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I call upon you, brethren, to mark those who the divisions and the stumbling-blocks, contrary to the teaching that ye did learn, are causing, and turn ye away from them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for such our Lord Jesus Christ do not serve, but their own belly; and through the good word and fair speech they deceive the hearts of the harmless,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for your obedience did reach to all; I rejoice, therefore, as regards you, and I wish you to be wise, indeed, as to the good, and harmless as to the evil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the God of the peace shall bruise the Adversary under your feet quickly; the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you. Amen!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salute you do Timotheus, my fellow-workman, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kindred;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I Tertius salute you (who wrote the letter) in the Lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: salute you doth Gaius, my host, and of the whole assembly; salute you doth Erastus, the steward of the city, and Quartus the brother,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to Him who is able to establish you, according to my good news, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the secret, in the times of the ages having been kept silent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now having been made manifest, also, through prophetic writings, according to a command of the age-during God, having been made known to all the nations for obedience of faith --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to him [be] glory to the ages. Amen.
